title: Signed negative rows can increase without tripping the compare gate
evidence: Reproduced. delta_rel forced 0.0 when neither pct positive -> -10%->-5% row 'stable' exit 0 despite +50% true relative change (and paradoxical top_regressions listing); slice+boundary paths, both languages. Fix: delta vs abs(before), +/-inf at zero baseline. Evidence: .goalloop-support/round3.triage.json#J1

title: Duplicate top-level report rows remain an order-dependent compare bypass
evidence: Reproduced. Duplicate top-level slice/boundary names last-wins -> same multiset reordered flips has_regression; order-dependent gate bypass in both languages. Fix: reject duplicate row names. #J2

title: Finite attributable inputs can silently become null in Rust
evidence: Reproduced. Finite attributable 1.7e308 scaled to inf: Python exit 2 raw json error, Rust exit 0 with null estimate (silent metric loss). Fix: shared finite-check + typed envelope both languages. #J3

title: Large valid integer totals break byte parity and by-slice selection
evidence: Reproduced. Python arbitrary-precision int division vs Rust f64-operand division diverges pct bytes and --by-slice selection for totals > 2^53; also serde_json default lossy float parse (needs float_roundtrip). Fix: Python mirrors Rust op order; enable float_roundtrip. #J4

title: Negative GC and uncollapsible pseudo-slices are silently discarded
evidence: Reproduced. GC and uncollapsible pseudo-slices gated > 0 drop valid negative aggregates in both languages, contradicting spec's negative-rows-rendered rule. Fix: != 0 like R2-07. #J5

title: String-field validation remains divergent on three public configuration surfaces
evidence: Reproduced. Non-string values coerced/dropped divergently on slice paths ([123]), target config patterns (123), rule match lists ([null]). Fix: reject non-strings, identical messages, both languages. #J6

title: Compare threshold flags accept different numeric grammars
evidence: Reproduced. --threshold-abs/rel grammars diverge: Python float() accepts '1_0'/' 2 ', Rust clap rejects. Fix: shared strict f64 grammar (Rust's) in Python. #J7

title: The Rust guide still says scope parity is not claimed
evidence: Reproduced. docs/CLANKERPROF_RUST.md still disclaims scope parity that PARITY.md and the shipped CLI provide. Fix: update stale text. #J8
